findings.description: The memo header fields (To, From, Subject) lose their tabular alignment in the HTML version, appearing as simple stacked lines rather than the structured label-value layout of the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'To:', 'From:', 'Subject:' as left-aligned labels with values indented to a consistent column. HTML shows them as left-aligned flowing text without the same structured indentation.

findings.description: The digital signature block appears in the HTML version between the From and Subject fields, which is additional content not visible in the first page of the PDF rendering.
findings.evidence: HTML page 1 shows 'Digitally signed by LYF / DN: cn=LYF, o=DWM, ou=SWS, email=larry.frost@ncdenr.gov, c=US / Date: 2016.05.12 12:11:52 -04'00'' between From and Subject fields. This information is not visible in the PDF page 1 rendering.

findings.description: The document has nested <main> elements. The 'MEMORANDUM' text is rendered as h1 with an underline style, which is acceptable as a document title, but it creates a slightly unusual structure with the actual section headings (I, II, III, IV) as h2.
findings.evidence:
HTML has

MEMORANDUM

as the top-level heading, with section headings as h2. The PDF presents 'MEMORANDUM' as a title and uses Roman numerals for section headings. This is an acceptable structural interpretation.

findings.description: The introductory paragraph from the cover page (memorandum body text) appears once in the HTML, but the cover page and page 2 both contain the same text in the PDF. The HTML correctly does not duplicate it, but the page header/footer info and the repeated memo header information from page 1 vs the body of page 1 are slightly interleaved.
findings.evidence: The memo 'From' block on page 1 of the PDF appears once in HTML, which is correct.

findings.description: The alt text for the logo image describes it as 'NC Waste Management Environmental Quality logo' which is a reasonable interpretation but not text explicitly present in the PDF.
findings.evidence: HTML: alt='NC Waste Management Environmental Quality logo'. The PDF shows the NC logo with text 'Waste Management' and 'ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY' beneath it. This is a reasonable descriptive alt text, not fabrication.
